task.title: Test a workflow in the interactive REPL
task.shortdesc: Testing a workflow requires validating that both the True and False branches execute correctly. Use the full md-validate validate command with representative documents rather than the REPL eval command, because eval tests only a single rule.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Verify that a markdown-validator workflow evaluates correctly by testing both branches against representative documents in the REPL.

prereq.p: Your workflow is written in the rule set file and you have at least two test documents: one that exercises the True branch and one that exercises the False branch.

step.cmd: Run validation on a document that should exercise the True branch of the workflow.

info.p: With --verbose, the output shows each rule evaluated and its result, including workflow steps.

step.stepresult: Confirm the workflow report shows the expected True-branch result for this document.

step.cmd: Run validation on a document that exercises the False branch.
step.stepresult: Confirm the workflow passes or fails as intended for this document type.

step.cmd: Use the REPL to inspect the metadata and structure of a document that produces unexpected results.

info.codeblock: md-validate repl > load docs/unexpected-result-doc.md > get ms.topic > query //h2 text
info.p: Compare the actual metadata and structure against what your workflow rules expect. Common causes of unexpected results:

ul.li: The precondition rule (the decision rule) matches more or fewer documents than you expected.
A sentinel rule (used in the False branch) is failing instead of always passing.
Whitespace in a metadata value causes an equality check to fail.
